description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging midsection, constructed from dark brown wooden staves. The barrel is reinforced by three wide, metallic hoops—one at the top, one at the bottom, and one around the center—each appearing to be made of steel or iron. The wood grain is visible, giving the surface a natural, slightly rough texture, while the metal bands have a matte, slightly reflective finish. The top and bottom surfaces are flat, with the top appearing to be sealed, suggesting the barrel is currently closed. There are no visible handles, spigots, or markings on the exterior. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage areas, or near taverns and markets.
description: A dark wooden barrel with three wide metal hoops and a sealed top, featuring visible wood grain and a slightly bulging shape. The surface is matte with no handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full curvature of the barrel, with all three metal hoops clearly visible and the wood grain texture prominent.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view, still showing the three metal hoops and the sealed top, with subtle changes in wood grain pattern.
description_view_2: Another rotated angle, maintaining visibility of the metal bands and the barrel's bulging midsection, with consistent wood texture.
description_view_3: Final view, similar to the others, highlighting the continuous metal hoops and the unmarked, sealed top surface.
